message: bd-db300.10.3: surface page buffer pool hot-path evidence

 {"id":"bd-db300.10.1","title":"J1: instrument baseline cache-hit, reuse, and hot-path waste rates for the non-structural engine gap","description":"## Goal
Produce the baseline-tax truthing layer that complements the structural truthing pass.

## Scope
- measure statement-plan cache hit rate, prepared-artifact reuse rate, decode-cache hit rate, cursor/frame reuse rate, page-buffer reuse, and other non-structural reuse signals
- attribute non-structural waste across parser/planner churn, VDBE setup, record decode, row materialization, PageData ownership churn, and transient allocation/copy cost
- focus on c1 rows, mixed rows with low retry pressure, and forced single-writer rows where baseline cost is easiest to isolate

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- representative low-retry rows expose stable cache-hit and reuse metrics plus a baseline waste ledger
- the ledger makes clear which costs are structural-convoy side effects and which are true baseline-tax problems
- unit tests cover metric aggregation and edge cases such as cold-cache startup versus steady state
- the deliverable includes a ranked summary of the dominant baseline waste buckets for c1, mixed low-retry, and forced single-writer rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- representative low-retry rows expose stable cache-hit and reuse metrics plus a baseline waste ledger
- the ledger makes clear which costs are structural-convoy side effects and which are true baseline-tax problems
- unit tests cover metric aggregation and edge cases such as cold-cache startup versus steady state
- the deliverable includes a ranked summary of the dominant baseline waste buckets for c1, mixed low-retry, and forced single-writer rows","notes":"Coordination note:
J1 should begin as soon as the A3 evidence leaves are ready; it must not wait for structural-truthing conclusions because the revised plan explicitly treats baseline tax as a parallel first-wave concern.

Verification/logging requirement:
- require a benchmark-output smoke check on c1, one mixed low-retry row, and one forced single-writer row
- record stable structured log families for baseline_waste_ledger, cache_hit_ledger, and allocation_copy_summary so later baseline beads can cite them directly
- ensure the resulting ledger separates true baseline waste from structural-convoy side effects instead of silently mixing them

User-facing deliverable:
- close the bead with a short ranked waste summary that tells future implementers where the biggest non-structural taxes actually are instead of leaving only raw counters","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:47.271218856Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:29:05.776188750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:29:05.775548451Z","close_reason":"Representative c1, mixed low-retry, and single-writer rows now produce stable baseline reuse and waste ledgers with ranked summary artifacts; downstream Track J beads can consume the concrete J1 evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","evidence","measurement","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:27:28.053459016Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:27:39.063674879Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:47.271218856Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2302,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers ES.2, P0.7, O.1 for the baseline track, and DG.4.
- This is the baseline-truthing bead that tells us whether c1 pain is still dominated by baseline tax after structural work.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:25Z"},{"id":2356,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: A3.3 is now closed and bv still points here as the next high-impact concrete task. br claim is currently blocked by the Track J parent-container edge (bd-db300.10), so I am recording work here explicitly and taking a narrow J1 slice anyway: inspect the existing hot-path profile surfaces and add the first machine-readable baseline cache/reuse/waste ledger fields in the fsqlite-e2e evidence path, starting with statement/prepare reuse, record-decode reuse, cursor/frame reuse, and PageData/data-motion signals where instrumentation already exists or can be added cheaply.","created_at":"2026-03-15T05:36:52Z"},{"id":2357,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: continuing J1 after re-running bv robot-next; current implementation slice is a first-class baseline cache/reuse/waste ledger in fsqlite-e2e using already-captured parser cache, compiled-plan cache, decode/materialization, and runtime phase signals. Agent Mail remains unavailable from this session, so Beads comments are the coordination fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-15T05:40:44Z"},{"id":2358,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: landed a concrete J1 slice in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs. actionable_ranking.json is now schema v3 and carries explicit baseline_reuse_ledger + baseline_waste_ledger entries built from existing parser cache, compiled-plan cache, decode/materialization, runtime-phase, and allocator signals. The new ledger explicitly separates baseline_tax, mixed_or_residual, and structural_side_effect lanes so low-retry rows can distinguish real non-structural tax from contention spillover. I also repointed parser/decode/materialization hotspot mappings at current Track J beads instead of stale D-lane beads, updated summary.md to surface the new sections, and extended hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts to assert the new JSON/markdown contract. Verification: rustfmt --check on perf_runner.rs passed; rch cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets passed; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts -- --nocapture passed. Remaining blocker to full bead close: still need representative real-row runs so the ledger is populated from c1 / mixed low-retry / forced single-writer benchmark evidence, not just the artifact smoke path.","created_at":"2026-03-15T05:57:43Z"},{"id":2362,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: proceeding with the remaining J1 acceptance work. The new ledger code is verified by rustfmt + rch cargo check + targeted hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts. Next slice is representative real-row evidence capture for c1, mixed low-retry, and forced single-writer rows so the ranked baseline waste summary is based on actual benchmark runs, not just the synthetic smoke fixture. Clippy remains blocked by the unrelated pre-existing pager lint at crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs:2449.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:08:22Z"},{"id":2363,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: the remote release-perf hot-profile attempt for representative J1 rows hit rch's 300s cap and died with exit 137 before artifact retrieval. Pivoting to a local incremental rebuild of realdb-e2e against /data/tmp/cargo-target so the three low-retry evidence rows can be captured directly without remote timeout noise.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:20:33Z"},{"id":2365,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: representative J1 evidence is now captured from real benchmark rows under artifacts/perf/20260315_j1_baseline_truthing. Ranked summary:
- commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c1 MVCC: statement_parse_cache 19/30 hits (63.33%), compiled_plan_cache 0/2 hits; top waste buckets are boundary_coordination (60.7 ms, mixed/residual), executor_body_residual (23.6 ms, mixed/residual), then baseline-tax buckets record_decode (0.388 ms), row_materialization (0.179 ms, 65.8 KB alloc pressure), parser_prepare_churn (0.173 ms).
- mixed_read_write c1 MVCC: statement_parse_cache 0/135, compiled_plan_cache 0/127; top waste buckets are executor_body_residual (9.13 ms, mixed/residual), boundary_coordination (5.99 ms, mixed/residual), then baseline-tax buckets parser_prepare_churn (4.75 ms, 7.2 KB alloc pressure), record_decode (3.92 ms), row_materialization (0.519 ms, 194 KB alloc pressure).
- mixed_read_write c1 single-writer: statement_parse_cache 0/135, compiled_plan_cache 0/127; top waste buckets are executor_body_residual (6.51 ms, mixed/residual), boundary_coordination (5.09 ms, mixed/residual), then baseline-tax buckets parser_prepare_churn (4.70 ms, 7.2 KB alloc pressure), record_decode (3.88 ms), row_materialization (0.457 ms, 194 KB alloc pressure).
- Unsupported-but-explicit reuse surfaces are now first-class outputs rather than silent gaps: cursor_frame_reuse (J7), page_buffer_pool_reuse (J3/J8), page_data_ownership_reuse (J6), and record_decode_cache (J5).
Verification: local debug realdb-e2e runs completed for the three representative rows; rustfmt --check on perf_runner.rs passed; cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets passed; targeted hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts passed. Clippy remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing pager lint at crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs:2449.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:28:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.10","title":"J10: verify baseline-tax reductions across c1, mixed, and single-writer rows with attribution-quality evidence","description":"## Goal
Force the baseline track to prove real wins rather than hiding behind architectural intent.

## Scope
- map each baseline implementation bead to concrete unit tests, e2e scripts, and structured log fields
- require before/after evidence on c1, mixed low-retry, and forced single-writer rows where baseline cost is easiest to isolate
- attribute observed wins, neutral results, and regressions to the specific baseline lever that caused them
- keep the result ready for the canonical matrix scorecard and the project-level narrative about where the remaining gap still comes from
- require exact rerun entrypoints and operator-facing triage steps for each baseline verification bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- every baseline implementation bead has named unit tests, named e2e scripts, expected artifact paths, and required structured log event names recorded
- the verification plan includes invalidation, reuse-boundary, reset/rollback, and stale-data negative cases where relevant
- attribution-quality benchmark artifacts exist for the major baseline levers
- the verification bundle makes clear which baseline ideas moved c1, mixed, or single-writer rows and which did not","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- every baseline implementation bead has named unit tests, named e2e scripts, expected artifact paths, and required structured log event names recorded
- the verification plan includes invalidation, reuse-boundary, reset/rollback, and stale-data negative cases where relevant
- attribution-quality benchmark artifacts exist for the major baseline levers
- the verification bundle makes clear which baseline ideas moved c1, mixed, or single-writer rows and which did not","notes":"Verification bundle requirements:
- for each baseline implementation bead, record the exact unit/property test modules or scenario families, the required e2e harness/script entrypoints, the expected structured log event families, and the artifact bundle keys or directories
- require before/after focused reruns for arenas/lookaside, page-buffer reuse, plan cache, decode cache, PageData churn, copy-heavy kernel reductions, VDBE fast paths, and perf-binary hygiene
- preserve negative and neutral outcomes with the same schema so the team can distinguish real wins from noise or optimism

Closure standard:
- a future agent should be able to rerun the baseline proof set from this bead and identify which levers moved c1, mixed, and forced single-writer rows without reopening planning documents","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:24.241164945Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T02:24:45.090977507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-j","e2e","logging","track-j","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:24.241164945Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:37.505007578Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:47.945672174Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:58.442745389Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:19:08.853822567Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:19:19.697266347Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:19:30.194380429Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:19:40.680629090Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:19:51.294983088Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:28:22.729195747Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:34:10.660315483Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:34:21.596327366Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2311,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.9 and the Phase-5 proof burden behind O.6.
- This is the bead that turns baseline-tax reductions into attribution-quality unit/e2e/logging/artifact evidence across c1, mixed, and forced single-writer rows.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:52Z"},{"id":2642,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Next-step note: c1 rerun/verification surface (bd-db300.10.10)

### What J10 needs to close

1. **Before/after c1 disjoint hot-path profile** — run `realdb-e2e hot-profile --workload commutative_inserts --concurrency 1` at the current HEAD and compare against the sqlite3 baseline. Exact rerun: `realdb-e2e hot-profile --workload commutative_inserts --concurrency 1 --bead bd-db300.10.10`.

2. **Before/after c1 mixed hot-path profile** — same as above with `--workload mixed_read_write --concurrency 1`.

3. **Before/after forced single-writer profile** — run with `--mode fsqlite_single_writer` to isolate baseline-tax from MVCC overhead.

4. **Per-lever attribution** — each closed baseline bead (program cache, copy-kernel, decode scratch, etc.) needs a delta_summary.json showing its individual contribution. This is the G5.4 proof-pack requirement.

5. **Rerun entrypoints** — every profile above needs an exact `rerun.sh` per the G6.3 artifact-manifest contract.

### What is NOT needed

- No new attribution archaeology — the hot-path profiling infrastructure already exists.
- No new framework — use the existing `realdb-e2e hot-profile` + `HotPathArtifactManifest` surface.
- No multi-lever bundles — each lever gets its own proof pack per G5.4 one-lever attribution gate.

### Blocking dependencies

- A7.2 (persistent 8t/16t packs) is IN_PROGRESS but J10 does not depend on it — J10 is c1-focused.
- G5.3 (evidence ownership) is CLOSED — provides the registry row schema.
- G5.4 (profile-first cards) is CLOSED — provides the proof-pack contract.
- G6.3 (artifact manifests) is CLOSED — provides the bundle shape.

### Minimum viable next action

Run the three profiles above (c1 disjoint, c1 mixed, c1 single-writer) at HEAD, emit HotPathArtifactManifest bundles, and compare against the sqlite3 baseline already captured. This gives the scorecard its c1 cells without requiring any new code.
","created_at":"2026-03-24T02:24:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.2","title":"J2: add statement and transaction-local arenas or lookaside storage for parser, VDBE, and materialization scratch","description":"## Goal
Reduce general-allocator churn on the hot execution path.

## Scope
- adopt explicit statement/transaction-local arenas or lookaside storage for parser objects, VDBE frame scratch, row materialization scratch, semantic/structural intent scratch where applicable, and other small transient buffers
- make ownership and reset boundaries explicit at reset/commit/rollback
- prefer wholesale arena drop/reset at lifecycle boundaries over repeated general-allocator churn for small transient objects
- add unit tests for reset safety, reuse safety, and no-leak/no-stale-data semantics
- add benchmark evidence showing lower allocation pressure and improved c1/mixed rows

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the targeted scratch/work objects move off the general allocator hot path
- reset/rollback semantics are explicit and covered by tests
- focused benchmarks show materially reduced allocation churn and improved baseline rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the targeted scratch/work objects move off the general allocator hot path
- reset/rollback semantics are explicit and covered by tests
- focused benchmarks show materially reduced allocation churn and improved baseline rows","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for arena/reset boundaries, rollback or abort cleanup, stale-data prevention, and nested statement/transaction lifecycle interactions
- add focused e2e runs that exercise repeated statements and mixed workloads with logs for arena_alloc_bytes, arena_reset_count, and fallback_to_general_allocator_count","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:57.815990752Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T11:35:30Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T11:35:30Z","close_reason":"Added statement-lookaside reuse/counters across parser and direct-insert scratch, covered reset/reuse safety with targeted core tests, and captured hot-path evidence exposing arena_alloc_bytes, arena_reset_count, and fallback_to_general_allocator_count in emitted artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","memory-management","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:57.815990752Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:16:44.125022935Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2303,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.1 and O.6.
- This is the arena/lookaside bead for removing general-allocator churn from parser/VDBE/materialization hot paths.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.10.3","title":"J3: add reusable page-image buffers and page-buffer pools for hot staging paths","description":"## Goal
Stop paying fresh allocation and ownership churn for common page-image staging paths.

## Scope
- introduce reusable page-image storage where safe, including per-thread or per-transaction page-image slabs when evidence supports them
- reduce repeated allocation and teardown of transient page buffers on hot write/read staging paths
- prefer reusable 4KB-class buffers and COW-friendly page-image reuse where possible rather than always allocating fresh owned images
- keep pool ownership and cross-thread safety explicit
- add tests for pool reuse, reset, contention safety, and no cross-transaction contamination

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- hot page staging uses reusable buffers or pools instead of repeated fresh allocation where safe
- tests prove no stale-data leakage or lifetime confusion
- representative rows show lower allocation/copy signatures than the baseline","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- hot page staging uses reusable buffers or pools instead of repeated fresh allocation where safe
- tests prove no stale-data leakage or lifetime confusion
- representative rows show lower allocation/copy signatures than the baseline","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for buffer reuse, reset-on-recycle, contention safety, no cross-transaction contamination, and any COW-friendly reuse boundary that is introduced
- add focused e2e or microbench runs that emit page_buffer_pool_hit, page_buffer_pool_miss, staged_page_copy_bytes, and page_image_slab_reuse_count before/after data
- keep logs specific enough that future PageData/data-motion work can reuse the same evidence shape
- closure notes should state whether the chosen design is per-thread, per-transaction, pooled across lanes, or a mixture, and why that balance was chosen","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:08.876644780Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:18.801063109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","page-buffers","pager","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:08.876644780Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:16:54.018684455Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2304,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.2 and O.6.
- This is the reusable page-buffer/page-image-pool bead, including slab/COW-friendly reuse variants.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.10.4","title":"J4: implement prepared-statement and execution-plan caching with explicit invalidation","description":"## Goal
Avoid re-parsing and re-planning repeated benchmark SQL where reuse is semantically safe.

## Scope
- add a statement/plan cache with explicit invalidation on schema or semantic changes
- support reset/rebind style reuse rather than rebuild-heavy lifecycles for repeated SQL strings
- include tests for invalidation, reuse, and correctness under repeated execution and schema churn
- emit logs and metrics showing cache hit rates and the share of statements served from reuse

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- repeated SQL can be served from a cached prepared artifact when valid
- invalidation semantics are explicit and tested
- focused benchmark rows show real hit rates and throughput improvements rather than speculative value","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- repeated SQL can be served from a cached prepared artifact when valid
- invalidation semantics are explicit and tested
- focused benchmark rows show real hit rates and throughput improvements rather than speculative value","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for schema invalidation, repeated execution reuse, parameter rebinding, and cache eviction boundaries
- add focused repeated-statement e2e runs that emit statement_cache_hit, statement_cache_miss, plan_cache_hit, invalidation_reason, and compile_reuse_count
- preserve correctness-first behavior: any case that cannot be reused safely must surface as an explicit miss reason, not hidden reuse","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:19.630882183Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T11:50:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T11:50:00Z","close_reason":"Promoted the existing prepared/compiled cache implementation to closure with explicit artifact surfacing for parser reuse and compile_reuse_count in hot-path reports, while retaining the existing connection.rs invalidation and reuse regression coverage for schema churn, repeated execution, parameter rebinding, and cache boundaries","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","planner","prepared-statements","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:19.630882183Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:04.034269370Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2305,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

Added coverage for reusable UPDATE/DELETE fast-path metadata and a prepared DELETE foreign_keys OFF->ON toggle regression. Tracker note: br update still rejects claiming this parent/child item with VALIDATION_FAILED / cannot claim blocked issue: bd-db300.10, so status could not be moved through normal claim flow.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:03:55Z"},{"id":2483,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-19 verification update: rch cargo check -p fsqlite-core --lib passed on the current source; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib test_prepare_update_with_params -- --nocapture passed after the prepare-time fast-path metadata change. A cold-worker run of test_prepared_delete_fast_path_rechecks_foreign_key_pragma_dynamically is still in flight; the code/test changes for that regression are already in the tree.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:08:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.5","title":"J5: add cursor-level record decode caching with explicit invalidation on page or image change","description":"## Goal
Stop repeatedly decoding the same row or cell representation when the underlying page image has not changed.

## Scope
- cache decoded record or row representations at the cursor level where safe
- invalidate on page/image movement or mutation
- add unit tests for cache hit/miss correctness and invalidation on write paths
- add mixed-row and c1 evidence showing fewer record decodes per returned row

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- decode-cache hits are observable in logs and metrics on representative workloads
- invalidation semantics are correct and covered by unit tests and mixed-workload e2e checks
- representative rows show materially fewer decodes and less decode-related cost","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- decode-cache hits are observable in logs and metrics on representative workloads
- invalidation semantics are correct and covered by unit tests and mixed-workload e2e checks
- representative rows show materially fewer decodes and less decode-related cost","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for decode-cache hit/miss correctness, invalidation on page/image movement, and invalidation on write paths
- add mixed-row and c1 runs that emit decode_cache_hit, decode_cache_miss, decode_invalidation_reason, and record_decodes_per_row
- ensure future agents can distinguish true decode wins from coincidental workload changes by artifact comparison alone","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:30.234087910Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:19.937393855Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","decoding","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:30.234087910Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:13.949971351Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2306,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.4 and O.6.
- This is the cursor-level record decode cache bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.6","title":"J6: audit and reduce PageData ownership transitions, full-page normalization, and clone-heavy representation flips","description":"## Goal
Remove representation churn that turns ordinary page movement into clone-heavy work.

## Scope
- inventory clone->vec->arc or equivalent ownership flips in the hot path
- reduce unnecessary full-page normalization before dirtiness or mutation is known
- keep representation changes explicit and justified when they remain necessary
- add focused tests and measurements proving the hot path performs fewer ownership transitions and less byte copying

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add focused tests around remaining representation-conversion boundaries so ownership simplification does not silently corrupt aliasing or lifetime assumptions
- add evidence packs that report pagedata_transition_count, full_page_normalization_count, and staged_copy_bytes on representative rows
- tie any remaining expensive conversions to an explicit justification in the bead closeout","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18.118250154Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-j","pagedata","pager","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:25.578635988Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:35.663600604Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2307,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.5 and O.6.
- This is the PageData ownership-transition / full-page-normalization reduction bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:41Z"},{"id":2458,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"PageData/baseline-tax note (2026-03-18):
- The prior instrumentation pass already landed PageData motion counters (`owned_passthrough`, resized copies, normalized payload bytes, zero-fill bytes) in the hot-path report pipeline. That work is the current measurement wedge for J6/J3.
- Fresh canonical c8 artifact capture with those counters is temporarily blocked by the `rch` 300s timeout on `release-perf` hot-profile replay, so there is not yet a new scored artifact pack tying PageData motion share to the current tree state.
- In parallel, I took a different bounded fixed-cost cut in `begin_concurrent.rs`: replace scan-based `release_all(txn_id)` with exact `release_set(...)` on the concurrent path when held-lock state is available. This is not a J6 change directly, but it helps separate MVCC coordination tax from PageData-motion tax so later J6 decisions are based on cleaner attribution rather than a blended microtransaction penalty.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:07:00Z"},{"id":2461,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18, pass 2):
- I re-read the landed PageData motion instrumentation in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` and the corresponding hot-path report wiring in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs` / `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs`. I did not find a concrete logic bug in the counters themselves during this pass.
- Fresh-eyes clarification: the new PageData motion report is allocator/copy evidence, not a standalone wall-time partition. The markdown/report wording already notes that component shares may overlap, so I left the semantics intact.
- This lane still needs a fresh artifact pack to become decisive, but the ranking/reporting plumbing now exposes owned passthrough vs resized-copy outcomes and total normalized bytes, which is the right evidence surface for J6/J3 once the remote replay blocker is gone.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18Z"}]}
